name: missions-test-suite
description: Build comprehensive validation contracts and testing infrastructure for the missions planning system, covering model integrity, plan structural validation, cross-feature assertion ownership, and full lifecycle end-to-end flows

milestones.name: model-validation
milestones.description: Validation contracts established for all mission data models — Mission, Feature, Milestone, and ValidationContract are tested for serialization, defaults, and edge cases

milestones.name: plan-validation
milestones.description: Planning validation engine verifies milestone references, feature dependency resolution, and assertion ID uniqueness; rejections are granular and actionable

milestones.name: infra-boundaries
milestones.description: Infrastructure boundary validation detects port conflicts, resolves service dependencies, and enforces off-limits resource policies

milestones.name: test-foundations
milestones.description: Test helpers, builder factories, and golden file infrastructure are reusable across the test suite, reducing boilerplate and improving assertion clarity

milestones.name: end-to-end
milestones.description: Full lifecycle flow from mission creation through validation and persistence is verified, including error paths and recovery

features.description: Implement validation contract model with assertion types, coverage metrics, and JSON round-trip serialization tests
expectedBehavior: ValidationContract marshals/unmarshals JSON without field loss
Assertion struct rejects empty ID and empty description
Coverage metric defaults to zero when omitted
Multiple validation contracts can be collected into a ValidationSummary

features.description: Implement Mission struct validation: required name, non-empty milestones, features reference existing milestones, unique feature IDs
expectedBehavior: Mission with empty name returns validation error
Duplicate feature ID across features returns validation error
Feature referencing nonexistent milestone returns validation error with milestone name
Valid mission with 3+ milestones and 5+ features passes validation

features.description: Implement Feature validation: required ID and description, milestone must exist in parent mission, preconditions reference existing feature IDs, circular dependency detection
expectedBehavior: Feature with missing ID fails validation
Circular precondition chain (feat-A → feat-B → feat-A) returns cycle error
Precondition referencing nonexistent feature returns targeted error
Feature with valid preconditions resolves into topological order

features.description: Implement assertion ID uniqueness validation across all features in a mission plan — each VAL-AREA-XXX assertion ID must be claimed by exactly one feature
expectedBehavior: Duplicate assertion ID across two features returns error with both feature IDs
Unclaimed assertion ID (in milestones but no feature) produces warning, not error
All assertion IDs in plan are claimed by exactly one feature after validation
Validation contract with assertion IDs can be cross-referenced against feature claims

features.description: Implement skill name validation — skillName must be one of the allowed values (implementation, qa, devops, documentation, architecture)
expectedBehavior: Feature with 'implementation' skillName passes validation
Feature with invalid skillName returns error listing allowed values
Empty skillName defaults to 'implementation'
Documentation skills can only be assigned to documentation-type features

features.description: Implement infrastructure boundary validation — service port conflict detection, off-limits resource enforcement, and dependency graph validation
expectedBehavior: Two services with same port return port conflict error with both service names
Service referencing off-limits port range (system < 1024) returns error
Service with unresolvable dependency returns error
Valid infrastructure plan with non-conflicting ports passes validation

features.description: Build test factory/builder helpers for Mission, Feature, Milestone, and ValidationContract to reduce test boilerplate
expectedBehavior: NewTestMission() returns valid mission with sensible defaults
NewTestFeature() auto-generates unique ID and references a valid milestone
Builder pattern supports override of any field
Helpers set up store with temp directory and return cleanup function

features.description: Implement golden file testing pattern for mission plan JSON output — generate golden files and provide update command
expectedBehavior: Golden file test compares plan JSON output byte-for-byte against stored golden
Update flag (-update) rewrites golden files when plan output changes intentionally
Missing golden file causes clear error mentioning -update flag
Golden files are stored alongside tests in testdata/ directory

features.description: Implement skip-condition test flag for unimplemented features — tests that verify future behavior skip with clear message instead of failing
expectedBehavior: Tests guarded by feature flag conditionally skip with t.Skip()
Skip message includes feature ID and expected milestone
Test runner reports skipped tests distinctly from passed/failed
Feature flags are centralized in a single location for toggling

features.description: Implement end-to-end test for full mission lifecycle: create → validate → persist → retrieve → validate again
expectedBehavior: CreateMission with valid plan returns mission with generated ID and timestamp
GetMission retrieves exactly what was created (deep equality)
CreateMission with invalid plan returns validation error, no persistence
UpdateMission preserves feature IDs and milestone references
DeleteMission removes mission and subsequent Get returns not-found error
Full round-trip plan survives JSON marshal/unmarshal without data loss

features.description: Implement recovery and error handling tests: partial writes, corrupted data, concurrent access
expectedBehavior: Corrupted mission file returns deserialization error with file path
Concurrent create and read of 10 missions completes without data race
Store with missing base directory auto-creates it on first write
Recovery from partial write leaves store in consistent state
